title: Add the invariant registry and registry-first read path
description:
Add `.jit/invariants.toml` as a registry-first item kind: a declarative registry of invariants, each with a `statement`, a `kind` of `enforced` or `advisory`, and an optional `enforced-by` binding to a rule or gate. The file loads at config time and invariants become indexed and queryable as a project-scoped kind addressed as `@/<self-id>`, exercising the registry-first source-of-truth direction (the counterpart to the markdown-first kinds).

Loading must happen on BOTH config-load return paths: the config-absent early return and the config-present path of `JitConfig::load` (`crates/jit/src/config.rs:432-451` and `:474`), so invariants load even when no `config.toml` is present. A new `invariants.rs` module mirrors `RuleSet::load` (`crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:902`). The registry is authoritative; there is no markdown index for invariants.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: An entry in `.jit/invariants.toml` declaring `statement`, `kind` (`enforced`|`advisory`), and optional `enforced-by` is loaded at config time.
- [hard] REQ-02: Invariants load on both config-load return paths, including when `config.toml` is absent.
- [hard] REQ-03: `jit item list --kind invariant` returns a loaded invariant, addressed as `@/<self-id>`.
- [hard] REQ-04: An invalid invariant entry fails config load with a typed, descriptive error.
- [hard] REQ-05: The registry is the authoritative source for invariants; no markdown index is produced for them.
